purpose: Labeled measuring stick for the two CALM-derived advisory bias checks added to cz_critique (D1). Authored BEFORE the detectors (L-39: build the adversarial fixture targeting the mechanism first). Each case carries the raw signal a self-critique is built from (a lesson's evidence field, a resolved open-item note, or a phase-summary line) and a human ground-truth label. The hardened rubric must surface the degenerate ones the PRIOR rubric scored clean, WITHOUT flagging the sound ones — including the adversarial near-misses, which a naive detector gets wrong.

labels.degenerate: the planted bias — the hardened detector SHOULD flag (expected = True)
labels.sound: no bias — the hardened detector should stay quiet (expected = False)
labels.neutral: factual prose, no over-claim (expected match = False)

note_on_near_misses: Cases marked near_miss=true are where a NAIVE detector errs: an external citation that is also in-repo-anchored (sound), a hollow note that is long (degenerate), a terse note that is anchored (sound), a factual 'all criteria' line with no intensifier (neutral). They are the precision test — the reason the gain is not a tautology.

authority.maps_to: D-017 evidence field on lessons; the prior Grounding check (_lessons_without_evidence) passes any evidence-BEARING lesson, so an unverifiable-authority citation slips through clean.
authority.principle: flag iff the evidence appeals to an external/unverifiable authority (arXiv/DOI/URL/'verified'/'the literature'/'standard practice') AND carries NO concrete in-repo anchor (commit SHA, src|docs|tests path, file.ext[:line], NNN tests/suite, a metric like nDCG/recall, a phase/date, an N/M run, a pinned CI leg).

cases.label: degenerate
cases.rationale: bare external paper id — nothing in-repo resolves it

cases.label: degenerate
cases.rationale: appeal to a vote; no checkable artifact

cases.label: degenerate
cases.evidence: as is well established in the literature

cases.label: degenerate
cases.rationale: external URL only; not in-repo provenance

cases.label: degenerate
cases.rationale: vague authority, no path or version

cases.label: degenerate
cases.evidence: obviously correct; this is standard practice

cases.label: degenerate
cases.evidence: per the CALM benchmark, this is the established best practice
cases.rationale: name-drops a benchmark but supplies no measured number/metric — still an unresolved appeal

cases.label: sound
cases.evidence: arXiv:2410.02736, validated in tests/test_critique.py against commit deadbeef
cases.rationale: cites a paper BUT anchors it in-repo — a naive 'contains arXiv' detector false-positives

cases.label: sound
cases.evidence: confirmed green on windows-3.11 CI, run 9/9
cases.rationale: uses the appeal word 'confirmed' but is anchored by a pinned CI leg and a concrete run ratio

hollow_resolution.maps_to: self-enhancement: the prior Coverage check only flags UNRESOLVED open items, so an item closed with a hollow self-serving note reads as clean.
hollow_resolution.principle: flag iff the resolution note matches a filler fingerprint ('done'/'looks good'/'handled'/'taken care of'/'working as expected'/...) AND carries NO in-repo anchor. Length is NOT the signal — a long filler note is still hollow; a terse anchored note is not.

cases.label: degenerate
cases.note: this has been fully taken care of and everything is now completely fine and working as expected
cases.rationale: LONG but entirely hollow — a length-threshold detector would wave it through

cases.label: sound
cases.note: added _authority_flags in critique.py:130; covered by test_authority_flags

cases.label: sound
cases.note: measured null: BM25 tied baseline at nDCG 1.0000, discarded

cases.label: sound
cases.rationale: terse but anchored — a length-threshold detector would false-positive

overclaim.maps_to: self-enhancement: in integration this fires only when a completion claim COEXISTS with a live objective gap; here we test the phrase detector in isolation.
overclaim.principle: match self-congratulatory intensifiers ('fully verified', 'no gaps remain', 'flawless', 'ready to ship', 'production-ready', '100%', 'everything works', 'complete and correct', 'comprehensive', 'nothing left to do'); a factual report of completion is NOT an over-claim.

cases.label: overclaim
cases.prose: Everything passes, fully verified, ready to ship.

cases.label: overclaim
cases.prose: All exit criteria met; no gaps remain; production-ready.

cases.label: overclaim
cases.prose: This phase is complete and correct - a flawless, comprehensive result.

cases.label: overclaim
cases.prose: 100% done, everything works, nothing left to do.

cases.label: neutral
cases.prose: Built the fixture; two checks prototyped; measurement pending.

cases.label: neutral
cases.prose: BM25 tied the baseline; recorded as a null result and discarded.

cases.label: neutral
cases.prose: Phase 0 done: added two helpers, updated one test, 622 tests green.
cases.rationale: 'done'/'green' are factual status words, not intensifiers

cases.label: neutral
cases.prose: All 4 exit criteria were checked off after verification against the labeled fixture.
cases.rationale: factual 'all criteria' with substantiation, no intensifier — a naive 'all.*criteria' detector false-positives
